column2: How much conflict of ideas is there in your work group? (Task Conflict)
How frequently do you have disagreements within your work group about the task of the project you are working on? (Task Conflict)
How often do people in your work group have conflicting opinions about the project you are working on? (Task Conflict)
How much relationship tension is there in your work group? (Relationship Conflict)
How often do people get angry while working in your group? (Relationship Conflict)
How much emotional conflict is there in your work group? (Relationship Conflict)
How often are there disagreements about who should do what in your work group? (Process Conflict)
How much conflict is there in your group about task responsibilities? (Process Conflict)
How often do you disagree about resource allocation in your work group? (Process Conflict)
I am satisfied with my present teammates
I am pleased with the way my teammates and I work together
I am very satisfied with working in this team
Being part of the team allows team members to do enjoyable work (Task Attraction)
Team members get to participate in enjoyable activities (Task Attraction)
Team members like the work that the group does (Task Attraction)
Team members like each other (Interpersonal Cohesiveness)
Team members get along well (Interpersonal Cohesiveness)
Team members enjoy spending time together (Interpersonal Cohesiveness)
Our team is united in trying to reach its goals for performance (Task Commitment)
I'm unhappy with my team's level of commitment to the task (Task Commitment) [scale reversed]
Our team members have conflicting aspirations for the team's performance (Task Commitment) [scale reversed]

column3: Everything is still going great. I would like to give special recognition to Nick West for being the primary team member who worked on the Aspen simulation. We could not have done it without him. I love my group.
I'm glad to be with this group and I think our project is coming along very nicely.
The team did exceptionally well this time around.

Amatul Salam was the leader for report 2. I had to remind her to contact the mentor and submit items on time. We had 2 canceled meetings with the mentor due to results not being submitted on time. I wish she was more on top as a leader and have hard deadlines for all group members. Our report was submitted late although I reminded her to submit on Sunday. Sean keeps preforming above expectations which I am impressed with. Brayden is sometimes absent from team work but submits his work on time for the most part. I felt like we didn't work as well as a team for report 2 but at the end of the day, we did good on our report and presentation, so I am thankful to my teams performance.
This team is perfect. Everyone helps each other and goes above and beyond the work.
I am happy with the work my team produces.
Team synergy has increased the more time we work together.
Our team is still doing well and getting along great.

On starting report 3, Agni has improved in her effort and there is better team cohesion. I feel as though going through the evaluation allows us to self evaluate and see what we can do to be better for and with our team mates. Tank you Betul.

For the most part, team is doing good but the only issue which we were running into was the uneven distribution of the work load. Me and Tyler did most of the work for our simulation by learning Super pro from scratch, Lihang tried as well but Cesar did not even bother to learn the simulator.

We have had disagreements in the group because it wasn't explicitly stated who was doing what for this report. But in the end things were finished and we were all happy for it coming together at the end. We already stated things were going to be done in a timely and efficient matter for the next report. But the way the other group members decided to deal with the situation was not professional or respectable. Hopefully moving forward we can come to a better way of dealing with our issues and work better as a group.
Everyone contributes their fair share as far as the work they put forth but I've been pleasantly surprised by what my teammates can accomplish when put to the task.
Very happy with the group.  Surpassed my expectations.

Liwei seems to be too busy with other classes to dedicate time for our group work. Responds to messages from the group but provides rushed, low-quality work. 

Awadh is great. Provides anecdotal industry experience, provides feedback on work, and is ALWAYS available to meet. However, he is lacking confidence in his work mostly due to the language barrier. Many times he has great ideas but feels he does not communicate them well. Also his technical skills can use some improvement, but he will not produce incorrect work. He is aware of not knowing something and seeks guidance to ensure he provides good quality work. 

Jason is reliable. Has adequate technical skills. Has a very fundamental chemistry view of the process. Loves to read much literature to fully understand what is going on. Good communicator and really binds the diverse group together. Only thing is he procrastinates quite a bit. No "complete" work is produced until he feels the pressure of the deadline approaching just a bunch of bits and pieces flying around. That was difficult for me as I am used to progressively completing chunks in full and putting them together.
Overall, good experience. Just especially thankful I had Miguel in the same group.
